most of my life. I've done just about any kind of job you can think of in construction.

your life would never be the same I believe it I'll bet you do think about them that often
I'm wondering, though, was that first encounter the most traumatic experience you've had with dogmen?

I mean, yeah, that one was pretty traumatic just because I had fell asleep.
Yeah, I knew I wasn't going to be able to get home in time.
So I knew it was going to get dark because I had fell asleep up there while deer hunting.
And when I got up, nothing was on my mind, really.
out to you know 15 20 steps and i don't know how they do it but it just fear and then immediately

It was dead silent.

I don't know if maybe we're the predator that sometimes causes it.

So that's just the way I live.

I just go hiking with my kids, and I try not to make it my everyday, like all day.

Oh, I'm sure it does.
Yeah, I don't doubt that at all.

You don't frighten easily.
The fact that you keep on going back and back and back looking for them.
That's all the proof that we need to know that you don't frighten easily.
From what I understand, Paul, your views have changed quite a bit since you came on the show for the first time.
Please expand on that for us.
Well, I had went down to this boat ramp with my girlfriend and my kids because people go down there and swim, you know, or swing, stuff to swing on.
Yeah, it's a good place to swim.
So we went down there and I just had a weird feeling out there because I had walked down the beach a little bit and I had found some really big dog tracks.
You know, of course I would walk down the beach a little bit and find a big dog track.
So, you know, naturally, I just pulled my phone out and recorded a couple of minutes of video there.
And when I watched it back, I started seeing orbs.
And when I say orbs, I'm like, people think, oh, it's probably a bug or something.
But these were lighting the leaves up as they went by them.
You know, it was very strange.
And I do believe that if anybody does any amount of research like I'm doing and reviewing footage and going out,
and anybody that does this, you're going to start to see the same things I'm talking about in the footage.
And you're going to come to the same conclusions.
Now, whether you ignore it or choose to go with it, like I just went with the evidence, I'm not going to.
It may sound crazy.
You know, I think it does too.
I know I'm not unaware of how loud some of it sounds, but it's absolutely the truth.
These things, there's other, I don't know if you want to call it cloaking, rounds, dimensions, whatever you want to call it.
But yeah, these things are able to interact and see us, but we can't see them and interact with them.
And every time I get a picture or a video of one of these things, I don't care how far away it is, it's always staring right at me.
And then I've got a lot of things on camera that are not Dogman, too.
So, you know, and it's really clear faces.
So, yeah, there's definitely another side to it.
But, you know, at the same time, I also believe that there's flesh and blood that can't do all that stuff.
I think the national parks would probably eat up with them.

you know and if you don't pay attention you'll be getting talked about on somebody else's podcast

and pretty much verified that, hey, we're here.

I became – I wouldn't say comfortable, but at peace with death.

Their sunglasses on, stared at me for probably two or three minutes before I got in my car and we decided to leave.

I've never seen them before, and I've never seen them since.
So, yeah, it was definitely something strange going on.
Something happened down there.

and I just can't in my mind picture that being a dog man screaming, you know,

When I go out, like a lot of times while I'm out there, I'm doing research.
I'm looking for stuff, tracks and all that.

I'm impressed.
